metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify where the content-predicate result enters the overlay_union formula: either add `diff_text` as a new input to the `overlay_union` step (with corresponding YAML wiring in both `triggers` and `union` steps) or introduce a dedicated pre-step that emits a boolean consumed by the union, and confirm the `overlay_union_input` schema and `workflow_ops.overlay_union` signature are updated accordingly.
findings.finding: The data-flow between the diff-content predicate and `overlay_union` is unspecified: `overlay_union` currently receives only `changed_files` (a list of file paths, not diff text), and calls `glob_triggered_overlays(changed)` which also operates purely on paths. A regex over '-' diff lines cannot fire unless the diff text (or a pre-computed boolean) is plumbed into the union step. The plan mentions `registry.glob_triggered_overlays seam` as the extension point but does not specify how the content-predicate result enters `overlay_union`'s formula (`(glob ∪ recommend) − already_run`).
findings.location: What / Scope sections; workflow_ops.py overlay_union step; code-review.yaml triggers step
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `content_triggered` input to `overlay_union` (parallel to `changed_files`); have `glob_triggered_overlays`-analog `content_triggered_overlays(diff_text)` return the list of triggered overlays; wire `diff_text: ${{ steps.assemble_diff.outputs.context }}` into both the `triggers` and `union` steps in `code-review.yaml`; extend `overlay_union` to union the content set: `selected = set(glob_set) | set(content_set) | set(recommend_ids)`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in Scope exactly where the content-trigger predicate integrates: name the new function (e.g., `content_triggered_overlays(diff_text) -> list[str]` in `registry.py`) and state whether `overlay_union` gains a new `diff_text` input or a separate pre-step injects results as synthetic `recommend` entries — this determines whether `overlay_union_input.schema.json` and `workflow_ops.py` are also edit targets.
findings.finding: The content-trigger seam (a diff-content predicate triggering an overlay) is entirely new behavior with no existing analogue in the codebase; it is a CREATION hunk that the plan classifies as in-scope, but the plan does not specify where the predicate logic lives (a new function in `registry.py`? a new module? a new `overlay_union` input field?), making the scope ambiguous for the `overlay_union` op and its input/output schemas.
findings.location: What section / Scope section / AC: 'Content-trigger seam added and unit-tested'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `code-review-base.md` (the overlay catalog prompt) to the plan's Scope and ensure AC-1 explicitly covers updating its overlay catalog bullet list, to satisfy the verified no-drift guard test (`test_base_prompt_enumerates_exactly_the_overlay_ids`).
findings.finding: The plan scopes 'registry.py, gates/code-review.yaml wiring, reviewers/code-review-deletion-impact.md, criteria_routing.json' but does NOT mention updating code-review-base.md. Yet a verified no-drift guard test (test_base_prompt_enumerates_exactly_the_overlay_ids at test_code_review_recommend_overlays.py:148-156) asserts that code-review-base.md's overlay catalog must enumerate EXACTLY the OVERLAY_IDS set. Adding 'deletion-impact' to OVERLAY_IDS without adding it to code-review-base.md's catalog will break this test.
findings.location: Scope section / tests/unit/test_code_review_recommend_overlays.py:148-156
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet to Scope: 'reviewers/code-review-base.md (add `deletion-impact` catalog entry)'. Update AC-1 to read: '`deletion-impact` added to the OVERLAY_IDS closed tuple, to code-review-base.md's overlay catalog, and to criteria_routing.json; dispatch reaches the overlay.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `reviewers/code-review-base.md` to the Scope's edit-target list (must add a `- \`deletion-impact\` —` bullet to the overlay catalog section to satisfy the no-drift guard test).
findings.finding: `code-review-base.md` must be updated whenever `OVERLAY_IDS` gains a new member (enforced by `test_base_prompt_enumerates_exactly_the_overlay_ids`), but the plan's Scope section does not list it as an edit target.
findings.location: Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Append `reviewers/code-review-base.md` (overlay catalog bullet) to the Scope file list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly call out in Scope that `gates/code-review.yaml` requires new `when: ${{ steps.triggers.outputs.include_deletion_impact }}` and `when: ${{ steps.union.outputs.include_deletion_impact }}` entries in the `round_a` and `round_b` batch criteria lists respectively.
findings.finding: The `round_a` and `round_b` batch steps in `gates/code-review.yaml` hardcode one `when:` line per overlay ID; adding `deletion-impact` to `OVERLAY_IDS` requires new `when:` entries in BOTH steps, but the plan's Scope only says 'gates/code-review.yaml wiring' without specifying these two concrete locations.
findings.location: Scope section / gates/code-review.yaml
findings.suggested_fix: Add to both batch step criteria lists: `- { prompt: code-review-deletion-impact, when: "${{ steps.<triggers|union>.outputs.include_deletion_impact }}" }`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `code-review-base.md` to the Scope and AC1: adding `deletion-impact` to OVERLAY_IDS requires a matching `- \`deletion-impact\` —` bullet in the base prompt's overlay catalog (enforced by `test_base_prompt_enumerates_exactly_the_overlay_ids`).
findings.finding: Adding 'deletion-impact' to OVERLAY_IDS without updating code-review-base.md will break the tool-verified no-drift guard test (test_base_prompt_enumerates_exactly_the_overlay_ids), which enforces that the base prompt's bullet catalog exactly equals OVERLAY_IDS.
findings.location: Scope section / AC1 ('REGISTER the overlay id')
findings.suggested_fix: Add 'code-review-base.md' to the Scope list and add a sub-task under AC1: 'Add `- \`deletion-impact\` — removed def/class/signature impact analysis` bullet to the recommend_overlays catalog in code-review-base.md'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify concrete additions to both round_a and round_b batch blocks in gates/code-review.yaml: `{ prompt: code-review-deletion-impact, when: "${{ steps.triggers.outputs.include_deletion_impact }}" }` and `{ prompt: code-review-deletion-impact, when: "${{ steps.union.outputs.include_deletion_impact }}" }`.
findings.finding: The gates/code-review.yaml batch blocks (round_a and round_b) hardcode one `when: include_<overlay>` entry per OVERLAY_IDS member; adding deletion-impact to OVERLAY_IDS without adding corresponding batch entries in both round_a and round_b means the overlay is never dispatched even when overlay_union emits include_deletion_impact=true.
findings.location: Scope section ('gates/code-review.yaml wiring') / AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope/AC1: 'Add `{ prompt: code-review-deletion-impact, when: ... }` entries to both round_a and round_b batch blocks in gates/code-review.yaml'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan how the content-trigger predicate output flows into the workflow: either extend overlay_union's input schema to accept diff_text (and update the triggers step's `with:` in code-review.yaml), or describe a new scripted step that runs the predicate and feeds the result into union.
findings.finding: The plan states the content-trigger predicate will live as a seam in registry.py alongside glob_triggered_overlays, but the plan never specifies how overlay_union will consume the new content-trigger signal. Currently overlay_union (workflow_ops.py:74-96) accepts only changed_files, recommend, already_run, and cap inputs. The overlay_union_output schema is described as 'Dynamic-keyed (one include_<overlay> per OVERLAY_IDS)' — but the trigger computation only calls registry.glob_triggered_overlays. There is no input path for a content-trigger result into overlay_union, and the gates/code-review.yaml wiring for the triggers step passes only changed_files. The plan's AC-2 says 'Content-trigger seam added and unit-tested; path-glob triggers unaffected' but does not specify how the content-trigger output is wired into the workflow.
findings.location: What section / AC-2 / src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py:59-96 / src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml:50-55

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the recall eval concretely: name a fixture set (e.g. N synthetic diffs with known dangling vs. updated callers), state a pass threshold (e.g. recall ≥ 80%, zero false-negatives on the rename case), and specify the command that runs it (e.g. `pytest tests/overlays/test_deletion_impact_recall.py`).
findings.finding: The 'dangling-deletion recall eval passes' criterion has no defined corpus, no pass threshold, and no concrete proving command — an agent cannot determine when this criterion is satisfied.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'dangling-deletion recall eval passes'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'Recall eval: a pytest fixture of ≥3 synthetic diffs (one dangling caller, one rename-with-update, one unrelated deletion) is included; `pytest tests/overlays/test_deletion_impact_recall.py` passes; dangling-caller case is flagged, rename-with-update case produces no finding.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a concrete eval dataset (e.g., N synthetic diffs with known dangling/non-dangling outcomes) and an explicit pass threshold (e.g., recall ≥ 90%, false-positive rate ≤ X%) so AC4 is objectively verifiable.
findings.finding: AC4's 'dangling-deletion recall eval passes' is not end-state-observable because no eval dataset, threshold, or pass/fail criterion is defined — an implementer cannot determine whether this AC is met.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'Recall eval: a synthetic suite of ≥5 diffs (at least 2 true-dangling, 1 renamed-with-callers-updated, 1 internal-only removal) run against the overlay; all dangling cases flagged, the rename case not flagged. Suite lives in tests/overlays/deletion_impact/.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly list as part of AC1/AC2 the three guard-test updates required: update `test_overlay_ids_is_the_expected_closed_set` to include `deletion-impact`, update `code-review-base.md` overlay catalog so the no-drift guard passes, and confirm `criteria_routing.json` entry satisfies `test_routing_index_covers_every_overlay_and_detector_keys`.
findings.finding: The plan does not mention updating the three existing closed-enum guard tests that will break as written when `deletion-impact` is added to `OVERLAY_IDS`: (1) `test_overlay_ids_is_the_expected_closed_set` asserts the exact 11-member set; (2) `test_base_prompt_enumerates_exactly_the_overlay_ids` asserts the base prompt lists exactly `OVERLAY_IDS`; (3) `test_routing_index_covers_every_overlay_and_detector_keys` iterates `OVERLAY_IDS` and asserts every member has a `criteria_routing.json` entry with required fields. All three fail without coordinated updates, yet the plan's test scope mentions only 'content-trigger seam added and unit-tested; path-glob triggers unaffected'.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (AC1, AC2); tests/unit/test_code_review_recommend_overlays.py:20-35, 148-155; tests/unit/test_code_review_ws2.py:19-34

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_code_review_recommend_overlays.py` (update `test_overlay_ids_is_the_expected_closed_set` to include `deletion-impact`) and `tests/unit/test_code_review_ws2.py` (update `test_routing_index_covers_every_overlay_and_detector_keys` and `test_overlay_prompts_resolve_as_code_review_pass_finders`) to the Scope's edit-target list.
findings.finding: The test `test_overlay_ids_is_the_expected_closed_set` asserts an exact 11-member set for `OVERLAY_IDS` and will fail the moment `deletion-impact` is added; the plan's Scope and AC do not enumerate this test file as a required edit target.
findings.location: Scope section / AC: 'Content-trigger seam added and unit-tested'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md section documenting the content-triggered overlay pattern — its interface, how it differs from path-glob triggers, and how future overlays should register new content predicates.
findings.finding: The content-triggered overlay pattern is a novel architectural decision (first non-path-glob trigger in the system) with no corresponding ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry.
findings.location: ## Scope — registry.py (trigger seam); ## What — content-triggered overlays

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify where the polyglot trigger regex lives (e.g., a config section in the overlay .md, a registry parameter, or a dedicated trigger-config file) so language-specific patterns can be updated without touching registry.py.
findings.finding: The polyglot regex trigger is described as 'expected to evolve per language' but the plan does not specify where the regex lives or how it is updated — if hardcoded in registry.py it cannot be tuned without a code change.
findings.location: ## What — deletion-impact overlay trigger description
findings.suggested_fix: Store per-language trigger patterns as a structured list in the overlay definition file (reviewers/code-review-deletion-impact.md front-matter or a companion JSON/YAML), loaded by the registry seam at startup. Document the update procedure.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the 'rename-with-callers-updated does not fire' check is encoded as an automated unit test (e.g., in tests/overlays/deletion_impact/test_trigger.py) so it runs on every CI pass.
findings.finding: AC4 states 'a valid rename-with-callers-updated does not fire' as a false-positive guard but does not specify how this is verified (unit test, integration test, manual check), leaving the non-regression proof unanchored.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'Verified by a unit test in tests/overlays/deletion_impact/test_trigger.py that feeds a diff with a renamed symbol and all call-sites updated, asserting the content-trigger predicate returns False.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an end-to-end acceptance check that feeds a synthetic diff containing a removed `def` through the full pipeline (trigger → dispatch → overlay → output) and asserts an advisory finding is produced; document the command (e.g. `pytest tests/integration/test_deletion_impact_e2e.py`).
findings.finding: No concrete proving command or end-to-end check is described for the full dispatch path: diff triggers predicate → overlay invoked → advisory finding emitted — so the union of steps does not reach the stated end-state.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria overall; 'What' section — full dispatch path

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the error-handling contract for the deletion-impact overlay: specify timeout, what the caller does on LLM/tool failure (suppress findings, log a warning, surface a 'could not complete deletion-impact analysis' advisory), and confirm the failure path never propagates a hard error to the review pipeline.
findings.finding: The plan introduces an agentic LLM call (the deletion-impact overlay) and file-search I/O (search_files / read_file) but specifies no error-handling, retry, timeout, or graceful-degradation behavior for failures in these operations.
findings.location: ## What — Caller-search mechanism; ## Acceptance Criteria — overlay is advisory
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap overlay invocation in a try/except that logs the failure and returns an empty findings list with a single advisory note ('deletion-impact analysis unavailable: <reason>'), preserving the no-block guarantee.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least a structured log line (or debug trace) for each content-trigger evaluation result (fired / not-fired) and for overlay execution outcome (success / error / skipped), so operators can monitor and tune trigger accuracy.
findings.finding: The plan adds a new trigger-fire path and an agentic overlay invocation but includes no observability (logging, metrics, or tracing) for overlay trigger hits, misses, or execution failures.
findings.location: ## What — content-trigger seam; ## Acceptance Criteria

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either remove the 'AST/LSP when available' claim and rely solely on search_files/read_file (which are verified to exist), or name the concrete integration path (e.g. a specific MCP server, tree-sitter binding, or jedi/pygls subprocess) with its availability precondition and fallback behavior.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'AST/LSP when available' as part of the caller-search mechanism, but the agent's tool surface (pai_tools.filesystem_tools) provides only read_file / list_directory / search_files — no AST tooling and no LSP client exist anywhere in the codebase. No integration path (MCP server, subprocess, library) is named, and no fallback is specified if AST/LSP is absent.
findings.location: What section — 'Caller-search mechanism (specified): the overlay uses its read-only tools (search_files / read_file; AST/LSP when available)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'AST/LSP when available' with 'search_files (substring, repo-root confined) with a fallback note that symbol precision is regex-level; a follow-on story may wire an AST/LSP MCP server when available' — this is honest about the tool surface and keeps the recall eval grounded.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the overlay's design: (a) which output schema `code-review-deletion-impact.md` is bound to, and (b) how the 'findings-only' constraint is enforced — either by using a schema that omits `recommend_overlays` or by an explicit instruction in the prompt.
findings.finding: The overlay is described as 'findings-only' but no output schema binding or structural enforcement mechanism is specified; if it is bound to the same code_review_base_output schema as other overlays (which includes recommend_overlays), nothing prevents it from escalating further overlays — violating the one-hop invariant.
findings.location: What section ('deletion-impact overlay (agentic, findings-only, one-hop)')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the overlay design (either in reviewers/code-review-deletion-impact.md front matter or in the plan's AC) what `criteria[]` values deletion-impact findings should carry and confirm the corresponding routing entry in criteria_routing.json.
findings.finding: The deletion-impact overlay's findings will carry a `criteria` field consumed by `threshold_for` for Pass-3 routing, but the plan never specifies what criterion id(s) the overlay should tag its findings with — leaving the Pass-3 block_threshold/blocking_enabled resolution undefined.
findings.location: What section / AC3 ('Overlay fires on removed def/class/signature lines')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fallback behavior for content-trigger predicate failure: document whether a regex/encoding error should return [] (fail-open, no deletion-impact overlay fires) or raise, and add this to the unit-test contract for AC2.
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for the new content-trigger seam when the diff-content predicate (polyglot regex) fails or produces an error — unlike the existing base_failure_result fallback for the LLM step.
findings.location: AC2 ('Content-trigger seam added and unit-tested') / What section
findings.suggested_fix: Adopt fail-open (return []) consistent with glob_triggered_overlays' pure-function contract; document this in the seam spec and test it explicitly.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
